intent: Trace POST workshop/api/shop/apply_coupon. The handler builds a raw SQL string by concatenating the user-supplied `coupon_code` (and `user.id`) into a cursor.execute call. Annotate the entrypoint that performs the raw query.

Trace GET workshop/api/shop/orders/{order_id}. The handler looks up Order by id; there is no @jwt_auth_required decorator and no ownership check, so any caller can read any order. Annotate the entrypoint.

Trace POST workshop/api/merchant/contact_mechanic. Follow `request.data['mechanic_api']` into the outbound `requests.get` call — the URL is taken directly from the request body with no host allowlist. Annotate the entrypoint.

Trace POST workshop/api/mechanic/signup. The handler is @csrf_exempt and not behind @jwt_auth_required; it creates a User with role=MECH gated only by a shared `mechanic_code`. Annotate the entrypoint and note the missing auth/role checks.

Trace GET workshop/api/management/users/all. The handler is decorated with @jwt_auth_required but performs no role/admin check before returning every UserDetails row. Annotate the entrypoint and note the absent role check.

Trace PUT workshop/api/mechanic/service_request/{service_request_id}. The handler updates ServiceRequest by id but never checks that the caller owns (or is the mechanic assigned to) that request. Annotate the entrypoint.

Trace GET workshop/api/mechanic/download_report. The handler validates `filename` against an allow-list, then `unquote()`s it (decoding `%2e%2e%2f` after validation) before joining with the reports directory and opening the file. Annotate the entrypoint and the validate_filename helper.

Trace POST workshop/api/shop/products. The handler is decorated with @jwt_auth_required but performs no role/admin check before saving a new Product to the catalogue. Annotate the entrypoint and note whether anything restricts who may create products.

Trace GET workshop/api/mechanic/receive_report. The handler has no @jwt_auth_required decorator and creates a ServiceRequest using the supplied mechanic_code + vin. Annotate the entrypoint and note the missing authentication.

Trace GET workshop/api/mechanic/service_request/{service_request_id}. The GET method has no @jwt_auth_required decorator (the sibling PUT does); any caller can read any service request by id. Annotate the entrypoint.

Trace POST workshop/api/shop/orders. Follow request.data['quantity'] through credit-balance arithmetic — there is no lower bound on quantity, so a negative value flips the available_credit subtraction into an addition. Annotate the entrypoint and (if a serializer enforces bounds) the validation function.

Trace POST workshop/api/shop/orders/return_order. The handler reads order_id from the query string but DOES compare order.user to the caller before returning. Annotate the entrypoint and note the present ownership check (Shape B negative case).

Trace GET workshop/api/mechanic/mechanic_report. The handler reads report_id from the query string, requires it to be numeric, and then fetches the ServiceRequest by id with no ownership check. Annotate the entrypoint and the service_report_pdf helper it calls.
